message:
feat(create): thread pre-opened storage context through the Create dispatch and auto-flush after each mutation

Two improvements to the `br create` command path:

1. Avoid re-opening storage on every Create. The main command dispatch
   pre-opens an `OpenStorageResult` once per process for commands that
   need it; every other mutating command already consumes the pre-opened
   handle, but Create was still calling `discover_beads_dir_with_cli()`
   and `open_storage_with_cli()` from scratch. On repeats (shell loops,
   batch scripts, test harnesses) that means redundant SQLite opens,
   duplicated config resolution, and duplicated workspace classification.
2. Flush to JSONL immediately on success. Previously Create returned
   after inserting, leaving the JSONL export stale until the next
   explicit `br sync --flush-only`. With auto_flush enabled in config,
   downstream tooling that reads issues.jsonl (agents, CI, bv) saw
   outdated state between Create and the next sync.

- src/main.rs: route `Commands::Create(args)` through the new
  `execute_create_command(args, overrides, output_ctx,
  &mut storage_result)` helper which `.take()`s the pre-opened handle
  and forwards it into `commands::create::execute_with_storage`. This
  transfers ownership so the same storage context cannot be reused by
  a later command in the same process — matching how Update, Delete,
  and friends already behave. Added
  `create_dispatch_reuses_preopened_storage_context` test covering
  the happy path (pre-opened handle is consumed, second db not opened).
- src/cli/commands/create.rs::execute_with_storage: accept the
  pre-opened handle as a new `Option<OpenStorageResult>` parameter and
  pass it down to `execute_import` so the `--file` import path gets the
  same optimization. When `None`, fall back to discover+open so ad-hoc
  callers still work.
- src/cli/commands/create.rs: add `auto_flush_after_create(storage_ctx)`
  helper that calls `OpenStorageResult::auto_flush_if_enabled()` and
  logs failures at debug level with beads_dir + error — failures are
  non-fatal so a flush issue never wrecks the user's create. Invoke it
  at the end of both the normal create path and the markdown-import
  path.
- src/cli/commands/create.rs::execute_import: accept and consume the
  pre-opened handle when present; otherwise fall back to discover+open
  (unchanged behaviour for older callers).
